refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single functional obligation: timely updating of the worksite model according to customer requirements. It does not combine multiple functions or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the latency of worksite model updates under supported scenarios and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) meets or is below the customer-defined target (e.g., ≤ 1.0 second).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.

candidate_dps: Incremental update mechanism that processes only changed data to minimize latency
Batch update mechanism that refreshes the entire worksite model at fixed intervals
Event-driven update mechanism triggered by detected changes in the worksite environment
Parallel processing of update tasks to meet timeliness requirements
Prioritization of critical update elements to ensure essential data is refreshed within latency constraints
selected_dp.selected_dp: Incremental update mechanism that processes only changed data to minimize latency

potential_sources: Integration with data sources providing update triggers
coupling_check.notes: The incremental update mechanism logically isolates update processing to only changed data, reducing coupling with full model refresh processes and supporting independence of update tasks.

assumptions_risks: Accurate and timely detection of changes in the worksite environment is available
Incremental updates can be applied without causing inconsistencies in the worksite model
Latency target of p95 ≤ 1.0 s is achievable with incremental processing under expected load
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining the change detection mechanism as a separate DP to further isolate responsibilities and reduce coupling.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core data processing and update mechanisms for the system.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives data updates and sends processed incremental updates.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles communication of incremental updates to other system components.

module_containment_scores.notes: The incremental update mechanism logically belongs here as it is central to data processing; minimal diffusion expected outside this module.
Vehicle module is more about physical control and state; incremental update logic would be diffused across other modules.
Teleoperation focuses on remote control commands, not data update processing; poor containment.
Safety Services are focused on alerts and safety monitoring, not data update mechanisms; very poor containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The incremental update mechanism is best contained within the NGHE Solution module, which is responsible for core data processing and update logic, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules if incremental updates affect their operation.
If incremental update logic grows to include safety-critical aspects, coordination with Safety Services may increase coupling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of an incremental update mechanism that processes only changed data directly addresses the FR of updating the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness by minimizing latency. This logical solution concept aligns well with the functional requirement and supports meeting the acceptance criteria of p95 latency ≤ 1.0 s.

diffusion_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules if incremental updates affect their operation.
If incremental update logic grows to include safety-critical aspects, coordination with Safety Services may increase coupling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The incremental update mechanism is well contained within the NGHE Solution module, which has a high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2). This indicates minimal diffusion risk and good architectural packaging. The module's scope encompasses core data processing and update mechanisms, supporting cohesion and ownership clarity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible since the NGHE Solution module interfaces with Telemetry and Network Link to receive and send incremental updates, enabling measurement of update latency against acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the FR to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and logical, as this module owns the core data processing and update logic necessary to fulfill the FR.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and manage interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to avoid increased coupling or diffusion as the incremental update mechanism evolves.
Establish coordination protocols with Safety Services if incremental update logic expands to include safety-critical functions to maintain modular integrity and reduce coupling risk.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is well addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. The incremental update mechanism logically satisfies the FR with acceptable latency, and the NGHE Solution module provides good containment and clear ownership. Although there are some diffusion risks related to interface complexity and potential coupling with safety services, these are manageable through monitoring and coordination protocols rather than requiring further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining modular integrity is crucial, but the current level of decomposition is sufficient to proceed with implementation and verification.

assumptions: The incremental update mechanism will remain focused on data processing and not expand significantly into safety-critical logic without proper coordination.
Interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules can be managed through established protocols.
open_risks: Potential increase in coupling if incremental update logic grows to include safety-critical functions without coordination.
Interface complexity may increase as the system evolves, requiring ongoing monitoring.
evidence_needed: Verification data showing update latency meets p95 ≤ 1.0 s.
Monitoring reports on interface interactions with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Documentation of coordination protocols with Safety Services if applicable.
